preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.19.2: TWO landed-together features — (A) window-level paste/drop into the chat composer (replaces the v1.19.1 bulk-upload tile), and (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap from BFF (worker secret = single source of truth). | (A) Bulk-upload UX refactor — kill the tile, embrace window-level paste/drop. The dedicated 'Bulk upload' Magic-panel tile (shipped in v1.19.1) is retired. Modern apps (Linear, Slack, ChatGPT desktop, Claude.ai, Cursor) all handle file input as window-level paste/drop into the composer; the LLM sees attachments alongside the user's typed context and decides routing — much smarter than a rigid filename Jaro-Winkler matcher running on every drop. New flow: (1) `window.addEventListener('paste', ...)` — clipboard image items materialize via the new `materialize-pasted-blob` IPC into `assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/<sha-prefix>_pasted_<unix>.<ext>` (16-hex SHA prefix mirroring bulk-upload's regression-guarded constant from PR #140; filename uses ZERO user input — pure SHA + literal `_pasted_<ts>` — eliminating injection risk); (2) `window.addEventListener('drop', ...)` — full-window drop overlay with dim+dashed-card aesthetic matching modal conventions; OS-path files chip directly without materialization; counter pattern (not boolean) prevents child-element flicker; (3) attachment chips above the composer — thumbnail (40×40 file:// for images, generic film-strip SVG for videos, generic image SVG for HEIC), filename truncated to 24 chars preserving extension, human-readable size, × button to remove; (4) on send, the chips' absolute paths get appended to the outgoing user message as an `Attached file:` / `Attached files:` block so Claude sees them and can `Read` each one. The IPC backend (`bulk-upload-assets` + Go Jaro-Winkler matcher) stays shipped, refactored into a reusable `runBulkUploadAssets` helper that's exposed to the new `mcp__merlin__bulk_upload` tool — the LLM calls it on demand when batch routing makes sense (5+ files + clear 'file these' intent) instead of on every drop. The merlin-content SKILL gets a 10-line note routing the agent: treat each attachment as direct content first (`Read` images, look at video), only call `bulk_upload` when the user explicitly asks for batch product association. Path safety: brand validated via existing `bulkIsValidBrand` regex; pasted-blob filename construction inspected for traversal (defensive `targetPath.startsWith(inboxDir + sep)` check despite no user-input flow); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist (image/png|jpeg|webp). Hook protection: `materialize-pasted-blob` writes only under `assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/` which is outside every PROTECTED_PATH_PATTERN entry in block-api-bypass.js. Tests: existing 26 bulk-upload + 15 oauth-persist + 29 captions all still pass; new app/paste-drop.test.js covers the pure helpers — MIME→ext mapping, size cap, brand-rejection passthrough, attachment dedup, sha-prefix consistency with BULK_SHA_PREFIX_LEN, attachment-message formatter (8 tests). Removed legacy: input-scoped paste handler that auto-fired 'I just pasted a video — saved at <path>. Take a look.' (replaced by the chip flow which lets the user type context first); chatEl drag-over CSS + JS handlers (replaced by window-level overlay). | (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap — every provider's clientID now flows from the BFF (Cloudflare Worker secret <FOO>_CLIENT_ID) through the exchange response into cfg.OAuthFoo.ClientID. Hardcoded values inside oauth.go's getFooOAuth factories become first-launch fallbacks only; the canonical value lives in one place — the Cloudflare secret. Closes the LinkedIn debug-loop class of incidents (2026-04-28: a redirect_uri mismatch surfaced 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the Cloudflare LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID secret?' as a suspicion that Wrangler couldn't answer because secrets are write-only). New: oauth_bootstrap.go pipeline (BFF response writes _oauthcreds_<provider>_clientID to the global vault, loadConfig rehydrates cfg.OAuthFoo on next startup), worker.js getOAuthClientIDs() emits an entry per provider in every exchange response (alongside service_tokens), 35-test oauth_bootstrap_test.go enforces drift-free correspondence between the binary's bootstrapProviderKeys + the worker.js map + the per-provider hardcoded fallbacks. Safety rails: BFF unreachable → factory falls back to hardcoded value (no crash, no refusal to start), ClientSecret is INTENTIONALLY never round-tripped to the binary (Hard-Won Security Rule 2 still holds — only the public ClientID flows). New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 17 documents the four required call sites for adding a new OAuth provider.
whatsNew: Paste screenshots or drag-drop files anywhere in the window — they attach to your next message as chips above the composer and Merlin decides what to do with them (Read the image, file it with a product, embed in an ad brief, whatever the context calls for).

Removed the dedicated Bulk upload tile from the Magic panel — the LLM is smarter about routing than a rigid filename matcher. The Go Jaro-Winkler matcher stays shipped, exposed as the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool that Merlin calls on demand when batch-filing 5+ files into product folders is clearly the right move.

Hardened (paste/drop chip flow): pasted clipboard images materialize to assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/ with a SHA-prefixed filename that contains zero user-controlled bytes (eliminates injection); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist enforced server-side; counter-based dragenter/dragleave prevents the flicker that single-boolean overlays produce when the cursor crosses child elements; attachment dedup so the same path never lands in the chip set twice.

Hardened: every OAuth provider's client_id now flows from the Cloudflare Worker secret as the single source of truth. The binary's hardcoded values are first-launch fallbacks only — the canonical value lives in one place, never two. Closes a class of debug loops where 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the secret?' couldn't be answered (Wrangler doesn't reveal secret values).

Hardened: 35 new Go tests (oauth_bootstrap_test.go) source-grep both the binary and worker.js to fail CI on any drift between the hardcoded fallbacks. Add a new OAuth provider in 4 places? CI checks all 4. Miss one? Test fails before merge.
